Bank of Ghana unveils new forex policy

The Bank of Ghana on Thursday announced a set of foreign-exchange measures aimed at easing pressure on the cedi.

Ad

The governor's office said the policy will take effect from August, with commercial banks required to report daily currency positions.

Share this article with your friends on social media platforms.

Analysts welcomed the move, describing it as a pragmatic response to recent volatility in the market.
